event: Mexican criminal networks begin trafficking China-sourced fentanyl to the U.S.
Heroin distributors in Tijuana and Mexicali begin adulterating doses with fentanyl. Nogales dealers offer M30 pills during heroin shortage.
Mexican criminal organizations establish large-scale fentanyl production, mainly from Sinaloa. Heroin trade collapses — opium prices crash.
National methadone shortage. Clinics close in Hermosillo (2015), Mexicali (2019), Nogales (2020). Patients pushed back to illicit market now contaminated with fentanyl.
Fentanyl consumption begins consolidating in Hermosillo. First contacts reported in Ciudad Juárez. SEMEFO Baja California begins testing cadavers for fentanyl in Mexicali.
Chapitos faction reportedly orders halt to fentanyl production (unconfirmed). SISVEA adds illicit fentanyl reporting code.
Users in multiple markets report declining fentanyl purity in pills. Possible fentanyl-stimulant mixtures detected in treatment centers across all five cities.
Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada captured in the U.S. Triggers violent factional war within Sinaloa Cartel across Sinaloa, Baja California, and Sonora.
Claudia Sheinbaum inaugurated as president of Mexico. Signals greater openness to security cooperation with the U.S.
Largest fentanyl seizure in Mexican history — 20 million pills in Sinaloa (Ahome/Guasave).
Salazar-Chapitos truce fractures in Hermosillo. Executions and disappearances of distributors and users increase. Fentanyl supply disrupted.
Mexico transfers 37 prisoners to the U.S. including major cartel figures. Sheinbaum launches 'Aléjate de las Drogas' anti-fentanyl campaign.
CJNG leader killed by Mexican military. Trump designates 6 Mexican criminal organizations as terrorists, classifies fentanyl as WMD.
Five people die in Ciudad Juárez from cocaine laced with fentanyl — first major polysubstance incident in the city.
Consumers in Nogales and Hermosillo report fentanyl scarcity — supply arrives in 'waves' with lower potency. Mexicali begins seeing similar pattern by late 2025.
U.S. fentanyl seizures at Mexico border drop 53% (Jan-June 2025 vs. 2024). U.S. overdose deaths fall ~27% in 2024 — largest annual decline ever recorded.
